---
title: "K-Means Clustering"
format: html
---

## Overview

K-Means is a centroid-based clustering algorithm that partitions observations into K groups by minimizing within-cluster variance. Each observation is assigned to the cluster with the nearest centroid.

## Parameters

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| K | 3 | Number of clusters to create |
| Max Iterations | 1000 | Maximum number of EM iterations |
| Seed | (random) | Optional random seed for reproducible results |

## When to Use

- You know (or can estimate) the number of clusters
- Clusters are roughly spherical and similar in size
- You want fast, deterministic grouping

## Output

K-Means produces hard cluster assignments — each frame belongs to exactly one cluster. No probability output is available.

Output is written as:

- **Entity groups**: One putative group per cluster (e.g., `"Cluster:0"`, `"Cluster:1"`)
- **Interval series**: Contiguous runs of each cluster assignment
- Colors are auto-assigned via the GroupManager

## Tips

- Try different values of K and compare the resulting groupings
- Enable z-score normalization if features have different scales
- Set a seed for reproducible results when comparing parameter settings
